question: Does the output explicitly cover all five traditional Chinese colors requested in the prompt: Yuebai (moon white), Xiang (pale buff yellow), Wan (light purplish-red), Dai (indigo black), and Zheshi (ochre)?

Does the response include all four required elements for each color: (1) an ancient Chinese style scene image, (2) etymology of the name, (3) mineral/plant dye sources, and (4) approximate color value range?
Does the text correctly distinguish the dye sources, identifying 'Dai' as derived from plant-based indigo (e.g., woad/polygonum) and 'Zheshi' as a mineral pigment (hematite/iron oxide)?
Does the text description for 'Yuebai' correctly identify it as a pale bluish-white (reminiscent of moonlight) rather than pure white?
Does the text correctly define 'Xiang' as a pale buff/light yellow (gardenia/zhe-wood source) and 'Wan' as a light purplish-red, avoiding modern neon or saturated interpretations?
Does the text provide specific numeric color value ranges (e.g., RGB, Hex, or HSL) for each of the five colors?
Does the generated image for 'Yuebai' accurately display a pale, slightly bluish-white color palette?
Does the generated image for 'Dai' accurately depict a deep indigo or blue-black tone?
Does the generated image for 'Zheshi' accurately display a reddish-brown or ochre earth tone?
Does the generated image for 'Xiang' accurately show a pale buff yellow tone?
Does the generated image for 'Wan' accurately depict a light purplish-red tone?
Are all generated images free from anachronistic elements (e.g., modern objects, Western architecture) and consistent with the requested ancient Chinese style?
Are the images free from severe AI distortions (e.g., malformed faces, incoherent objects) and of sufficient resolution to clearly identify the color?
Is the content presented in an interleaved format where each image is placed immediately adjacent to the text describing that specific color?
Do the visual colors in the images match the approximate numeric color value ranges provided in the corresponding text descriptions?
Does each image feature the specific traditional color as a dominant or clearly highlighted element, serving as an informative visual reference?
